title: Telemetry JSON payload is constructed via string interpolation without escaping
reasoning: CHANNEL (from PRISM_CHANNEL env var) and VERSION (from env var or remote JSON parsed via sed on line 112) are interpolated directly into a JSON string without escaping quotes or backslashes. A value containing a double-quote character breaks the JSON structure or injects arbitrary fields into the telemetry payload sent to the API endpoint. While this is a self-inflicted issue for local env vars, a compromised or misconfigured release metadata endpoint (line 112) could supply a VERSION containing quotes.
reproduction: PRISM_VERSION='1.0","injected":"true' bash scripts/install.sh — observe malformed JSON in the POST body.
recommendation: Use a tool like jq or bun -e to construct the JSON payload safely, e.g., bun -e 'console.log(JSON.stringify({installId:process.argv[1],...}))' or escape special characters before interpolation.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are included; exploiting requires setting PRISM_VERSION or PRISM_CHANNEL to a value with embedded quotes.
suggestedRegressionTest: Shell test: set PRISM_VERSION to a string containing a double-quote, run the payload-construction logic in isolation, and validate the output is well-formed JSON via a JSON parser.
minimumFixScope: Replace the manual JSON string construction on line 252 with a call to bun -e or jq that properly serializes the values.
